source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
In the diagram on the right the following can be seen: a straight line, which is the common tangent of two touching circles with radius 1, and a square with one edge on the straight line and the other vertices one on each of the two circles. How big is the side length of the square?
<image1>

answer letter A was converted to the corresponding option text

image_dependency: question explicitly says 'In the diagram on the right' and asks about a geometric configuration shown there
the setup depends on spatial relations among a tangent line, two touching circles, and a square with vertices on the circles
multi_step: solving requires combining multiple geometric conditions: common tangent, two radius-1 circles touching, and square placement
the target quantity is not directly stated and likely needs construction/relationship setup plus algebra or geometry to derive the side length
verifiability: answer is a precise closed-form value ('2/5') with answer_type=short_text
multiple-choice options are provided, so the final result can be checked against a fixed candidate set
registry_passed=true, answer_present=true, and the image asset exists
